exemplar.title: How does Madeline Miller use syntax and diction to characterize Achilles and Patroclus through the evolving dependency in their relationship in The Song of Achilles?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does Madeline Miller use syntax and diction to characterize Achilles and Patroclus through the evolving dependency in their relationship in The Song of Achilles?

subject.title: English Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Literature focuses exclusively on the study of literature, developing students' appreciation and critical analysis of literary works from various cultures, periods, and genres. Students examine poetry, prose, and drama through close reading, comparative analysis, and exploration of literary techniques and themes. The course cultivates sophisticated interpretive skills and deep understanding of how literature reflects and shapes human experience across different cultural contexts.

strengths: Clear and consistently communicated focus on syntax and diction as tools of characterisation
Research question is explicitly stated and remains central to the essayâs structure
Introduction and thesis restatements show deliberate planning and a clear purpose
weaknesses: Occasional repetitive phrasing slightly detracts from clarity
Methodology is generally appropriate but lacks justification for source selection
Limited range of secondary sources reduces methodological depth
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the investigation maintains a precise and purposeful focus and the research question is both clear and well-integrated, the methodology strand is only âmostly complete.â The limited secondary source range and absence of selection rationale place this criterion solidly in the 3â4 band, meriting a final score of 4.

strengths: Demonstrates solid knowledge of The Song of Achilles with accurate contextualization
Consistent and accurate use of a range of literary terms (anaphora, tricolon, juxtaposition)
Effective close-reading of key quotations deepens textual insight
weaknesses: Secondary material is thinly integrated and sometimes cited without page references
Application of sources to argument is generally effective but not consistently seamless
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay shows good understanding of the novel and employs terminology accurately, aligning with the upper 3â4 band. However, uneven integration of secondary sources and occasional citation lapses prevent this from reaching the 5â6 band, justifying a score of 4.

strengths: Perceptive observations of how syntax shifts reflect character development
Well-chosen primary quotations that reinforce relevance to the research question
Insightful comments on diction (e.g., sensory imagery in the fire-to-sleep passage)
weaknesses: Overall evaluation remains at a surface level with limited engagement in counter-argument
Some analysis lists effects without probing their broader significance
Lacks sustained critical evaluation of the authorâs broader achievements and limitations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research is relevant and analysis is generally supported by evidence, placing the work in the 4â6 band. However, the discussion and evaluation remain partially critical rather than fully analytical, so the thesis earns a mid-band score of 4 in Critical Thinking.

strengths: Logically organized structure with a comprehensive table of contents and clear section headings
All required elements (title page, introduction, conclusion, works cited) are present and well-ordered
weaknesses: Inconsistent in-text citation formats (e.g., unclear bracketed references)
Erratic symbols (e.g., âktb121â) and typographical errors interrupt professional presentation
Pagination is irregular and formatting conventions (headers/footers) are inconsistently applied
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Structure fully meets requirements (band 3â4) but formatting and layout exhibit multiple lapses (band 1â2). The combined performance across these two strands merits 3 out of 4 for Presentation.

strandSelections.reasoning: The methodology (close textual analysis supported by limited secondary criticism) is generally appropriate: quotations are embedded, rhetorical devices are named, and a short works-cited list is supplied.  However, the range of sources is modestâone primary text plus five secondary items, two of which are web articles of doubtful academic weight.  There is little evidence of selection rationale or of alternative critical perspectives.  The approach is âmostly completeâ rather than âfullyâ so, justifying a 3-4 band; the stronger textual focus earns the top of that band (4).

Formatting conventions are mostly met: title, word count, section headings and table of contents are present.  However, some in-text citations are inconsistent (use of brackets like â(45, 5)â without clear reference system), and line-breaks plus erratic symbols ($ktb121$) interrupt professional layout.  These partial lapses place the work in the 1-2 band, with a score of 2 indicating that most but not all requirements are observed.

Primary quotations are plentiful and well-selected; the few secondary sources, while limited, are relevant to the focus on modern retellings and classical heroism.  Research therefore shows âgoodâ rather than âexcellentâ breadth.  Because relevance is high but range moderate, the mid-band (4-6) is appropriate, with a score of 5 reflecting the stronger end of that band.

The introduction immediately identifies Madeline Millerâs use of syntax and diction as the lens of investigation and links this to the changing dependency between Achilles and Patroclus.  The student restates and refines this focus several times, signalling a deliberate, clearly-defined purpose that guides the whole essay.  Though wording is occasionally repetitive, the topic remains precise and never drifts into general thematic commentary.  Overall clarity and focus therefore sit securely in the 5-6 band, with minor lapses preventing the top mark.

An overall line of argument is evidentâfrom initial contrast, through mutual growth, to final humanisationâbut evaluation of Millerâs success and of wider implications remains surface-level.  Counter-arguments and critical self-reflection are absent.  Consequently the discussion fits âgood argument development with partially critical evaluation,â warranting a lower 4 within the 4-6 band.

The student moves beyond paraphrase to examine how sentence length, verb choice and rhetorical devices convey character.  Most claims are linked to textual evidence and logically explained.  Nonetheless, analysis sometimes becomes descriptive (listing effects without probing their significance) and lacks engagement with alternative interpretations.  This âgood, mostly supportedâ standard sits comfortably at 5 in the 4-6 band.

The essay contains all required elementsâtitle page, contents page, labelled sections, introduction, conclusion, works citedâand these follow a logical progression tied to the thesis.  Headings and pagination (implied by the contents list) aid navigation.  Therefore the structure is âclearly appropriate,â justifying the top of the 3-4 band.

The essay demonstrates good knowledge of The Song of Achilles, supplying accurate plot context and well-chosen quotations.  Secondary materials are understood but thin; citations occasionally lapse (e.g., stylistic claims without page numbers).  Application of source material is generally effective yet not consistently integrated into argumentation.  These traits align with âgood knowledge â¦ partially effective application,â placing the work at the upper end of the 3-4 band.

Literary terminologyâanaphora, simile, tricolon, declarative syntax, fragmentation, juxtapositionâis employed accurately and consistently.  Concepts such as heroic archetype, vulnerability, and classical heroism are used with confidence.  Occasional minor slips (ânormal cyâ) do not impede clarity.  Overall use is âaccurate and consistent,â meriting a score of 5 within the 5-6 band.

The research questionââHow does Madeline Miller use syntax and diction to characterise Achilles and Patroclus â¦ ?ââis explicitly stated on the title page and reiterated in the introduction.  It is narrow, literary-technique driven, and wholly appropriate for English A.  Subsequent sections (Initial Characterization, Comfort Zones, etc.) map directly onto the question, showing a strong connection between RQ and discussion.  Minor awkward phrasing in the question mark after the novelâs title slightly detracts from polish, so a solid 5 is awarded.

annotations.comment: Consider breaking the very long first sentence into two to improve readability and maintain focus.
The research question is clearly and prominently stated, providing precise focus on syntax and diction in characterisation, which effectively guides the essayâs direction.
Including the word count at the start meets formatting requirements, demonstrating attention to IB guidelines.
The unexplained inline code âktb121â interrupts professional presentation; remove or clarify this symbol to maintain formality.
The table of contents is comprehensive and clearly structured, enhancing navigation and reflecting a logical progression.
Several page numbers in the TOC appear misaligned within their cells; ensure accurate tabular formatting to avoid confusion.
Page numbers in the header/footer appear inconsistently placed or missing; standardize header/footer placement across pages.
The opening paragraph succinctly contextualizes Millerâs retelling and demonstrates strong knowledge of the primary text.
The citation âDushin, 2022â lacks page reference; include specific page numbers to strengthen academic rigour.
The thesis restatement at the bottom of page 4 refines focus and outlines methodology clearly, signaling effective structural planning.
The subtitle âSection I: Initial Characterizationâ effectively signals a clear structural division tied to the research question.
The parenthetical citations (1,1) and (6,2) lack clear bibliographic linkage to Works Cited style; adopt a consistent citation system.
Strong use of literary terminology (anaphora, tricolon) demonstrates mastery of subject-specific concepts.
Some descriptive passages risk listing effects without deeper interpretive insight; probe why these devices shape character, not just how.
Claim about âsupernatural auraâ is insightful but would benefit from contrasting citation of a secondary critic to deepen analysis.
The observation that sentence length shifts with character proximity is perceptive, indicating nuanced critical thinking.
Typographical error ânormal cyâ likely meant ânormalcyâ; correct to avoid distracting reader.
The quotation sequence from The Song of Achilles is well-chosen to illustrate syntactical shifts, reinforcing relevance to RQ.
The truncated continuation tag âhas-continuationâ suggests missing text; verify that no critical analysis is omitted.
The introduction of Section II is concise and clearly signals thematic progression, maintaining essay coherence.
Repeated use of âMillerâ to start sentences creates monotony; vary subject or employ pronouns to improve style.
Detailed examination of present participles demonstrates effective use of terminology and deepens textual insight.
The link between quotations and analysis is sometimes implicit; explicitly state how syntax effects shape character perception.
Insightful observation on Patroclusâs shift to assertiveness shows strong critical engagement with character evolution.
Long descriptive paragraphs occasionally overwhelm the reader; consider tighter paragraphing to enhance clarity.
The analysis of sensory imagery in the fire-to-sleep passage is perceptive, reflecting nuanced understanding of diction.
The term âTheraponâ is well contextualized, but defining it within the text is more effective than relying on parentheses.
The shift from descriptive analysis to evaluative commentary on classical heroism is an effective higher-order insight.
Works Cited entries lack consistent formatting (e.g., punctuation, italics) and uniform style; apply a recognised citation style guide.
Consider reflecting on challenges encountered and how they shaped argument development to strengthen engagement reflection.
